IRMAA applies?

    Compare scenarios (estimated yearly total)

    Estimates only. This is an educational model using 2026 figures and typical copays — not a quote. Your actual costs depend on the specific plan, your providers, prescriptions, and area. Confirm with Medicare.gov or a licensed agent.

    Understanding Medicare Costs

    Premiums

    The monthly amount you pay to have coverage — Part B, plus Part D and/or Medigap or a Medicare Advantage plan.

    Deductibles

    What you pay out of pocket before your coverage starts paying (e.g., the annual Part B deductible).

    Copays

    A fixed dollar amount you pay for a service, like $10 for a doctor visit.

    Coinsurance

    A percentage you pay for a service — Original Medicare's Part B coinsurance is typically 20%.

    Out-of-pocket maximum

    Medicare Advantage plans cap your yearly in-network medical costs. Original Medicare has no cap, which is why many people add Medigap.

    Extra Help

    A federal program that lowers Part D prescription costs for people with limited income and resources.

    IRMAA

    An income-related surcharge added to Part B and Part D premiums for higher-income beneficiaries.

    Questions about Medicare costs?

    A licensed Utah agent can pressure-test these numbers against real plans — free, no pressure.

    Call (801) 368-0700 Text Us
    Free Medicare Review

    Frequently Asked Questions

    How much does Medicare cost?
    Most people pay $0 for Part A and a standard Part B premium ($202.90 in 2026). Total costs depend on the plan you choose, your prescriptions, and how often you use care.
    What are out-of-pocket expenses?
    What you pay beyond premiums — deductibles, copays, and coinsurance when you receive care.
    What is IRMAA?
    An income-related surcharge added to your Part B and Part D premiums if your income is above a set threshold.
    What is Extra Help?
    A federal program that lowers Part D prescription drug costs for people with limited income and resources.
    How much do Medigap plans cost?
    Medigap premiums typically range from about $120 to $250 per month depending on the plan letter, your age, and your area.
    Do Medicare Advantage plans have maximum out-of-pocket limits?
    Yes — they have a yearly in-network maximum out-of-pocket limit that caps what you pay for covered medical services.
